title: horminum coccineum
intro: Horminum coccineum, also known as Digitalis purpurea, is a flowering plant in the foxglove family that is native to Europe. The plant contains digoxin and digitoxin, cardiac glycosides with medicinal properties that have been used to treat various heart conditions, including atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure. The health benefits of Horminum coccineum include improved cardiovascular function, increased energy, and reduced symptoms of heart failure. Horminum coccineum can be prepared as a herbal remedy in the form of dried leaves, tinctures, or extracts, which are often used to support heart health and treat various cardiovascular conditions.
